function.name: execute
function.description: Execute a shell command inside the Kali Linux sandbox. All recon tools are pre-installed.

command.description: Shell command to run inside sandbox.

function.name: advanced_fuzz
function.description: Run advanced heuristics and intelligent fuzzing for Zero-Days. Uses fuzzer.py internally.

function.name: run_parallel_agents
function.description: Launch multiple parallel AIRecon scout agents on multiple targets simultaneously, bounded by a concurrency semaphore.

targets.description: List of targets/domains to scan in parallel.

prompt.description: The exact prompt/directive for the parallel agents to execute on each target.

function.name: browser_action
function.description: Control a headless browser to navigate, interact, or extract data from websites.

action.description: The action to perform. SCREENSHOT — screenshot: take a screenshot of the current page; returns screenshot_path (saved to workspace/output/). Use to visually inspect a page, verify login state, or capture CAPTCHA images manually. AUTH WORKFLOW — always read 'next_action' field in every response for guidance: (1) login_form → response has: login_success, captcha_detected, captcha_type, captcha_screenshot (auto-taken), mfa_required, login_error, next_action. (2) If captcha_detected=true: CAPTCHA screenshot already taken automatically (see captcha_screenshot path) → call request_user_input(input_type='captcha', prompt='Solve CAPTCHA in <path>'). (3) If mfa_required=true AND you have secret: handle_totp(totp_secret=BASE32SECRET) → check totp_success. If no secret: request_user_input(input_type='totp'). (4) For username-first flows (Google/GitHub/Microsoft): login_form(multi_step=true). (5) After success: save_auth_state. (6) Restore session: inject_cookies → goto URL. (7) OAuth/SSO: oauth_authorize(url=..., callback_prefix=...). (8) check_auth_status: verify if currently logged in (returns is_authenticated, confidence, username_display). (9) wait_for_element: wait for CSS selector to appear/disappear (use after SPA transitions or login redirects).

parallel.description: For action=execute_js only: when true, run JS concurrently across all open tabs (do not set tab_id).

username.description: Username or email for login_form action.

username_selector.description: CSS selector for username/email field in login_form (comma-separated fallbacks). Default covers: input[type=email], input[name=username/user/email/login/mail], input[autocomplete=username/email], #username/#email/#user/#login. Override when default fails — first call view_source to find the correct selector.

password_selector.description: CSS selector for password field in login_form. Default: input[type='password']. Override for non-standard forms.

submit_selector.description: CSS selector for submit button in login_form (comma-separated fallbacks). Default tries: button[type=submit], input[type=submit], button:has-text('Log in/Login/Sign in/Continue/Next/Submit'). Override when default fails.

totp_secret.description: Base32 TOTP secret for handle_totp action. Generates a fresh RFC 6238 code automatically. Example: 'JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP'. If rejected, call handle_totp again immediately (new 30s window). For 8-digit codes use totp_digits=8. For 60s period use totp_period=60.

multi_step.description: For login_form: set true for username-first flows (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, enterprise SSO) where password field only appears AFTER submitting the username. Workflow: fill username → click Next → wait for password field → fill password → submit.

totp_digits.description: For handle_totp: number of digits in the TOTP code. Default: 6 (standard RFC 6238). Use 8 for enterprise apps that require 8-digit codes.

totp_period.description: For handle_totp: TOTP time step in seconds. Default: 30 (standard). Use 60 for older/non-standard apps with 60-second windows.

wait_selector.description: For wait_for_element: CSS selector to wait for. Example: 'div.dashboard', '#logout-button', '.error-message'.

wait_timeout.description: For wait_for_element: timeout in seconds. Default: 10.

wait_state.description: For wait_for_element: element state to wait for. Default: 'visible'.

cookies.description: Array of cookie objects [{name, value, domain, path}] for inject_cookies action.

oauth_url.description: OAuth authorization URL for oauth_authorize action.

callback_prefix.description: Expected callback URL prefix for oauth_authorize to detect redirect completion.

function.name: request_user_input
function.description: Pause the agent and request user-provided input (e.g., CAPTCHA, TOTP/OTP, password, or manual confirmation) before continuing.

prompt.description: Clear instruction to the user explaining exactly what to enter. Example: 'Please enter the 6-digit TOTP code from your authenticator app for target.com' or 'Solve the CAPTCHA shown in screenshot_20240101_120000.png and enter the text here'.

input_type.description: Type of expected input. 'totp' = 6-digit time-based code, 'captcha' = CAPTCHA solution text, 'otp' = one-time password (SMS/email), 'password' = sensitive credential, 'text' = anything else.

timeout_seconds.description: How long to wait for user input in seconds. Default: 300 (5 minutes). Use shorter timeout for time-sensitive inputs like TOTP.

function.name: create_file
function.description: Create a new file in the workspace. Overwrites if exists.

path.description: File path relative to the workspace root (e.g. 'output/results.txt'). Do NOT include a leading 'workspace/' prefix — it is stripped automatically.

content.description: Content to write to the file.

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a file from the workspace with optional offset/limit pagination for large outputs.

path.description: File path relative to the workspace root (e.g. 'output/nmap.txt'). Do NOT include a leading 'workspace/' prefix — it is stripped automatically.

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (0-indexed). Default: 0 (start of file).

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return. Default: 500. Use smaller values for very large files.

function.name: list_files
function.description: List files and directories in the workspace with metadata; prefer this over execute ls.

path.description: Directory path to list (relative to workspace, e.g. 'output/', 'tools/', or leave empty for the target root). Default: target root.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web via SearXNG (DuckDuckGo fallback) for payloads, CVEs, bypasses, and testing references.

max_results.description: Maximum results to return (default: 10, max: 50). Use higher values for thorough dorking.

function.name: create_vulnerability_report
function.description: Create a vulnerability report. Enforces duplicate-title checks; include required PoC fields for valid submissions.

title.description: Clear, specific title (e.g., "SQL Injection in /api/users Login Parameter"). But not too long. Don't mention CVE number in the title.

description.description: Comprehensive description of the vulnerability and how it was discovered

impact.description: Impact assessment: what attacker can do, business risk, data at risk

target.description: Affected target: URL, domain, or Git repository

technical_analysis.description: Technical explanation of the vulnerability mechanism and root cause

poc_description.description: Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the vulnerability

poc_script_code.description: Actual proof of concept code, exploit, payload, or script that demonstrates the vulnerability. Python code.

remediation_steps.description: Specific, actionable steps to fix the vulnerability

attack_vector.description:
CVSS Attack Vector — how the attacker reaches the vulnerability.
N=Network: exploitable remotely over the internet (SQLi, XSS, SSRF, IDOR, open redirect, API vuln).
A=Adjacent: requires attacker on same local network/VLAN (ARP spoofing, internal-only API).
L=Local: requires local OS access/shell (privilege escalation, local file read).
P=Physical: requires physical device access.
Most web vulnerabilities = N.

attack_complexity.description:
CVSS Attack Complexity — how reliably the attack can be repeated.
L=Low: attack works consistently with no special conditions (SQLi, IDOR, open redirect, auth bypass, XSS, path traversal, most web vulns).
H=High: requires specific conditions outside attacker control (race condition with narrow window, MitM required, target must have specific non-default config).
Default for most web vulns = L.

privileges_required.description:
CVSS Privileges Required — authentication level needed BEFORE exploiting.
N=None: no login required (unauthenticated endpoint, public API, login-page SQLi).
L=Low: any authenticated user can exploit (IDOR between regular users, stored XSS by normal user).
H=High: only admin/privileged role can exploit (admin panel XSS, admin-only IDOR).

user_interaction.description:
CVSS User Interaction — must a VICTIM perform an action for the exploit to work?
N=None: attacker exploits directly with no victim interaction (SQLi, SSRF, IDOR, path traversal, auth bypass, server-side injection, unauthenticated RCE).
R=Required: victim must click a link, visit a page, or take an action (reflected XSS, CSRF, open redirect, phishing-based attacks, DOM XSS via crafted URL).
IMPORTANT: SQLi/SSRF/IDOR = N. Reflected XSS/CSRF/open redirect = R.

scope.description:
CVSS Scope — does the vulnerability's impact extend beyond the vulnerable component?
U=Unchanged: impact stays within the vulnerable component/service (SQLi, IDOR, path traversal, auth bypass, reflected XSS same-session, open redirect, most web vulns). DEFAULT for most findings.
C=Changed: attacker can impact OTHER components or OTHER users beyond the vulnerable service (stored XSS that steals cookies of OTHER users in their sessions, SSRF that reaches internal network services, XXE with OOB that contacts external systems, privilege escalation to OS level).
WARNING: Do NOT use C just because the vuln is serious. Reflected XSS = U. Stored XSS affecting other users = C. Open redirect = U. SSRF to cloud metadata = C.

confidentiality.description:
CVSS Confidentiality Impact — what sensitive data can be read?
N=None: no data read (pure injection with no data leak, availability-only impact).
L=Low: some non-critical info exposed. Use L for: username enumeration, version disclosure, error stack traces, path disclosure, email addresses, public user metadata, wp-json user listing, HTTP headers leaking framework info.
H=High: sensitive/private data compromised. Use H for: password/credential theft, session token theft, full database dump via SQLi, private user data (SSN, payment info), source code disclosure, admin data access via IDOR.
DO NOT use H for username enumeration or version disclosure — these are L.

integrity.description:
CVSS Integrity Impact — what data can be modified?
N=None: attacker cannot modify any data (read-only IDOR, info disclosure, open redirect).
L=Low: limited data modification (minor stored XSS affecting only attacker's own account, parameter tampering with bounded effect).
H=High: critical data/config can be modified or created (admin account creation, password change of any user, RCE writing files, mass data modification, SQLi UPDATE/DELETE, CSRF changing sensitive settings).

availability.description:
CVSS Availability Impact — can the attacker disrupt service?
N=None: no availability impact (information disclosure, redirect, read-only IDOR — the vast majority of web vulns).
L=Low: degraded performance or intermittent service interruption.
H=High: full service crash or DoS (RCE killing the process, ReDoS causing 100% CPU, billion laugh XML, GraphQL DoS query).
Most web vulnerabilities = N for availability.

endpoint.description: API endpoint(s) or URL path(s) — for web vulns, or repo path — for code vulns

method.description: HTTP method(s) (GET, POST, etc.) — for web vulnerabilities.

cve.description: CVE identifier (e.g., "CVE-2024-1234"). Verify it's valid via web_search before using.

suggested_fix.description: Optional: A concrete code snippet or configuration change showing how to fix the vulnerability.

flag.description: CTF flag value (e.g., FLAG{example}). Use this field when reporting a CTF challenge flag.

function.name: quick_fuzz
function.description: Run a fast fuzz scan on a URL using common payloads (SQLi, XSS, traversal, SSTI) with automatic parameter targeting.

params.description: Optional list of parameters to fuzz. If omitted, defaults to common params: q, search, id, page.

function.name: deep_fuzz
function.description: Run thorough interactive fuzzing with chain discovery after initial hints from quick_fuzz or advanced_fuzz.

params.description: Optional list of parameters to fuzz. If omitted, auto-discovers high-priority targets.

vuln_types.description: Optional vulnerability types to test. If omitted, tests all available types.

function.name: generate_wordlist
function.description: Generate a targeted payload/parameter wordlist from internal data and save it to the workspace for ffuf/feroxbuster.

output_file.description: Filename to save the wordlist (e.g. 'sqli_payloads.txt'). Saved inside output/ folder.

vuln_types.description: Filter payload categories to include. Available: sql_injection, xss, command_injection, path_traversal, ssti, xxe, ssrf, idor, mass_assignment, parameter_pollution, jwt, graphql, race_condition. Omit to include ALL categories.

max_combinations.description: Cap on parameter name mutation variants appended after payloads (default: 300, max: 1000).

function.name: caido_list_requests
function.description: Query Caido history with HTTPQL filters and return request IDs, methods, paths, and statuses.

function.name: caido_send_request
function.description: Replay or modify HTTP requests through Caido using a request_id or raw HTTP payload.

request_id.description: Caido request ID to replay (from caido_list_requests). Optional if raw_http provided.

raw_http.description:
Full plain-text raw HTTP request to send (e.g. 'GET /api/user?id=2 HTTP/1.1
Host: target.com

'). Do NOT base64-encode — executor handles encoding.

port.description: Target port (default: 443 for TLS, 80 for plain)

function.name: caido_automate
function.description: Start a Caido Automate fuzzing session using §FUZZ§ markers to define injection points.

raw_http.description:
Full raw HTTP request with §FUZZ§ markers at injection points (e.g. 'POST /login HTTP/1.1
Host: target.com

{"user":"admin","pass":"§FUZZ§"}')

payloads.description: List of payloads to inject at §FUZZ§ positions (e.g. ["' OR 1=1--", "admin", "<script>alert(1)</script>"])

function.name: caido_get_findings
function.description: Fetch findings/annotations stored in Caido, including title, description, reporter, and related request data.

function.name: caido_intercept
function.description: Control Caido intercept: check status, pause/resume capture, list queued messages, forward (optionally modified), or drop.

action.description: Action to perform: status (get intercept state: RUNNING/PAUSED), pause (stop capturing), resume (start capturing), list (show queued messages), forward (forward a queued message), drop (discard a queued message)

message_id.description: ID of the intercept message to forward or drop (from list action)

raw_http.description: Modified raw HTTP request to send when forwarding (optional — omit to forward unchanged)

function.name: caido_sitemap
function.description: Browse Caido sitemap nodes (hosts/directories/endpoints); pass parent_id to drill into child nodes.

parent_id.description: Node ID to list children for (from a previous caido_sitemap call). Omit to list root hosts.

function.name: caido_set_scope
function.description: Set Caido proxy scope to focus capture and testing on specific hosts. Allowlisted hosts are captured; denylisted hosts are excluded.

function.name: spawn_agent
function.description: Spawn a specialist subagent for deep investigation of a specific finding/endpoint and merge results back to the session.

task.description: Specific investigation task for the subagent (e.g. 'Test /api/login for SQL injection, parameter: username')

target.description: Target URL or domain for the subagent (e.g. 'https://target.com/api/login')

specialist.description: Type of specialist: sqli (SQL injection), xss (Cross-site scripting), ssrf (Server-side request forgery), lfi (Local file inclusion/path traversal), recon (Reconnaissance only), exploit (General exploitation), analyzer (Code/config analysis), reporter (Report generation)

function.name: schemathesis_fuzz
function.description: Run schema-aware API fuzzing with Schemathesis against OpenAPI/Swagger specs to detect crashes, auth bypasses, and injections.

schema_url.description: URL or file path to the OpenAPI/Swagger spec (e.g. 'https://target.com/openapi.json', 'https://target.com/swagger.yaml', '/workspace/target/output/api-spec.json'). Common paths to try: /openapi.json, /swagger.json, /api/docs, /api-docs, /v1/openapi.json.

base_url.description: Base URL of the API to test against (e.g. 'https://target.com/api/v1'). If omitted, uses the server URL from the schema.

auth_header.description: Authorization header value to include in every request (e.g. 'Bearer eyJhbGc...' or 'Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz'). Use session auth_tokens if available.

checks.description: Schemathesis checks to run. Available: not_a_server_error, status_code_conformance, content_type_conformance, response_schema_conformance, negative_data_rejection. Omit for all checks.

max_examples.description: Max test cases per endpoint (default: 30). Increase for deeper coverage, decrease for speed.

function.name: code_analysis
function.description: Run Semgrep static analysis on source code in the sandbox to detect security issues (OWASP/CWE patterns).

target_path.description: Path to scan inside the workspace (e.g. 'output/repo-clone', '.' for current target root). Relative to the target workspace directory.

rules.description: Semgrep rule sets to use (e.g. ['p/security-audit', 'p/owasp-top-ten']). If omitted, uses all default security rules.

languages.description: Filter to specific languages (e.g. ['python', 'javascript', 'java']). If omitted, scans all detected languages.

function.name: http_observe
function.description: Send raw HTTP requests from the sandbox and return full response details; supports baseline save and response diffing.

method.description: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS. Default: GET

headers.description: Additional request headers as key-value pairs (e.g. {"Authorization": "Bearer token", "Content-Type": "application/json"})

save_as.description: Name to store this response as a baseline (e.g. 'baseline_normal', 'baseline_auth'). Stored in session for later diffing.

compare_to.description: Name of a previously saved baseline to diff against. Shows what changed between the baseline and this response.

follow_redirects.description: Whether to follow redirects. Default: false — keep false to observe Location header for redirect-based vulnerabilities (open redirect, SSRF via redirect).

function.name: record_hypothesis
function.description: Create or update a security hypothesis with status (pending/testing/confirmed/refuted) and optional evidence.

claim.description: The security hypothesis claim. E.g. 'The /api/user endpoint is vulnerable to IDOR via user_id parameter' or 'Login form is vulnerable to SQLi in username field'.

test_plan.description: What specific test/payload to run to verify or refute this hypothesis. E.g. 'Send GET /api/user?user_id=2 while authenticated as user 1. If response returns user 2 data, IDOR is confirmed.'

status.description: Current status: 'pending'=not yet tested, 'testing'=currently verifying, 'confirmed'=vulnerability proven, 'refuted'=tested and not vulnerable.

hypothesis_id.description: ID of an existing hypothesis to update (e.g. 'h_5_2'). If omitted, a new hypothesis is created.

evidence.description: Evidence supporting or refuting the hypothesis. Include HTTP response snippets, diff results, or tool output that proves/disproves the claim.

phase.description: Pipeline phase where this hypothesis was formed (RECON/ANALYSIS/EXPLOIT). Default: current phase.
